phases.name: State and Decoder Foundation
phases.description: Extend StoryState and ChoiceData for Tier 2 state fields; add NodeKind.readCount and InkDecoder CNT? handler.

steps.name: Add chosenChoiceTargets and ChoiceData flags
steps.description: Extend StoryState with chosenChoiceTargets: Set<String> (Codable, decodeIfPresent default []) and extend ChoiceData with flags: Int (Codable, decodeIfPresent default 0). Extend CodingKeys on both types. No engine behaviour changes in this step.
steps.criteria: StoryState encodes and decodes chosenChoiceTargets as Set<String> using decodeIfPresent defaulting to []; ChoiceData has flags: Int decoded with decodeIfPresent defaulting to 0; existing StoryState JSON without chosenChoiceTargets round-trips without error
steps.implementation_notes: Add chosenChoiceTargets: Set<String> field to StoryState struct with init value []. Add chosenChoiceTargets case to CodingKeys enum. In init(from:) use decodeIfPresent([String].self) then convert to Set, defaulting to []. In encode(to:) encode as sorted Array for deterministic output. Add flags: Int to ChoiceData struct with CodingKeys addition; use decodeIfPresent defaulting to 0. Both changes must be backward-compatible with pre-Tier-2 saved states.
quality_gates: swift build succeeds with no new warnings
existing StoryState Codable round-trip tests pass
StoryState decoded from JSON lacking chosenChoiceTargets field does not throw
deliverables: StoryState.chosenChoiceTargets: Set<String> field with Codable support
ChoiceData.flags: Int field with Codable support
Backward-compatible decode for both new fields

steps.name: Add NodeKind.readCount and InkDecoder CNT? handler
steps.description: Add .readCount(String) case to NodeKind enum. Update InkDecoder.classifyDict to match {CNT?: key} dict nodes before the unknown-fallback path, returning .readCount(key) instead of falling through to .controlCommand("CNT?").
steps.criteria: A {"CNT?": "cafe"} dict node is classified as .readCount("cafe") not .controlCommand("CNT?"); all existing decoder tests still pass
steps.implementation_notes: In NodeKind.swift add case readCount(String) after the existing cases. In InkDecoder.classifyDict, add a guard/if before the fallback: if the dict has exactly one key "CNT?" and its value is a String, return .readCount(value). This guard must appear before any generic fallback that would produce .controlCommand. The classifyDict method is called from classify(_:) for [String: Any] elements.
quality_gates: swift build succeeds with no new warnings
NodeKind switch exhaustiveness check passes (all existing dispatch sites compile)
Existing decoder unit tests pass unmodified

phases.name: Engine Logic
phases.description: Implement once-only suppression, conditional gating, visit count increment and read, and invisible default auto-divert in InkEngine and TreeWalker.

steps.name: Once-only suppression in collection loop
steps.description: In InkEngine.stepToNextLine choice-collection loop, skip a choice when flags & 0x10 != 0 AND target path is in state.chosenChoiceTargets. In chooseChoice(at:), record the target path in state.chosenChoiceTargets for every choice whose flags & 0x10 != 0. Set ChoiceData.flags from the in-flight choice node during collection.
steps.criteria: Once-only choice that has been picked is absent from currentChoices when story loops back; sticky choices remain present after being picked; picking one once-only removes only that choice not others; all Slice 01 acceptance tests pass
steps.implementation_notes: During choice collection, capture the choicePoint node's flags value and store it in ChoiceData.flags. Add suppression guard: if (flags & 0x10 != 0) && state.chosenChoiceTargets.contains(targetPath) { skip }. In chooseChoice(at:), after resolving the chosen ChoiceData, if choiceData.flags & 0x10 != 0 insert choiceData.targetPath into state.chosenChoiceTargets. chosenChoiceTargets is already Codable (01-01), so suppression survives save/restore automatically.
quality_gates: all 8 Slice 01 acceptance tests pass (7 in-memory/save-restore + 1 oracle on macOS)
swift build succeeds with no new warnings
no regression in Tier 1 acceptance tests
deliverables: Once-only suppression guard in stepToNextLine collection loop

steps.name: Conditional gating in collection loop
steps.description: In InkEngine.stepToNextLine choice-collection loop, when flags & 1 is set, pop the boolean from state.evalStack. If the popped value is false, skip the choice. The pop must always occur (even when skipping) to keep the evalStack balanced.
steps.criteria: Choice with false condition is absent from currentChoices; choice with true condition is present; unconditional choices always appear regardless of variable state; all Slice 02 acceptance tests pass
steps.implementation_notes: In the choice-collection loop, after reading flags from the choicePoint node: if (flags & 0x01 != 0), pop the top InkValue from state.evalStack and call .asBool. If the result is false, skip appending the choice but continue the loop. If evalStack is empty when a pop is expected, treat as false and continue (defensive handling). This guard runs independently of the once-only guard from 02-01; order: conditional gate first, then once-only gate.
quality_gates: all 5 Slice 02 acceptance tests pass (4 in-memory/save-restore + 1 oracle on macOS)
evalStack is always balanced after collection loop regardless of choice outcome
no regression in Slice 01 or Tier 1 tests
deliverables: Conditional gating guard in stepToNextLine collection loop
Unconditional evalStack pop when flags & 0x01 is set

steps.name: Visit count increment and readCount dispatch
steps.description: In TreeWalker.dispatch, add a case for .readCount(key) that pushes state.visitCounts[key] ?? 0 as .int onto state.evalStack. In the container entry path, increment state.visitCounts[containerPath] when the container's #f flag has bit 0x1 (CountVisits) set.
steps.criteria: Visit count for a location increments each time that location is entered; visit count is queryable during story execution; visit counts survive save/restore; all Slice 03 acceptance tests pass
steps.implementation_notes: Add case .readCount(let key): state.evalStack.append(.int(state.visitCounts[key] ?? 0)) to the dispatch switch in TreeWalker. For container entry: InkEngine already resolves containers by path; add a hook at the point a container is entered to check container.flags & 0x1 != 0 and if so perform state.visitCounts[resolvedPath, default: 0] += 1. The containerPath string must match the key format used by CNT? nodes in inklecate-compiled JSON — validate against real slice03 fixture. visitCounts is already in StoryState and Codable.
quality_gates: all 5 Slice 03 acceptance tests pass (4 in-memory/save-restore + 1 oracle on macOS)
TreeWalker dispatch switch remains exhaustive (compiles without warning)
no regression in Slice 01, Slice 02, or Tier 1 tests
deliverables: visitCounts increment on container entry when #f bit 0x1 is set

steps.name: Invisible default auto-divert
steps.description: In InkEngine.stepToNextLine choice-collection loop, detect invisible defaults (flags & 0x17 == 0) and record the first such target in a local pendingInvisibleDefault variable without appending to currentChoices. After collection, if currentChoices is empty and pendingInvisibleDefault is non-nil, apply that divert and continue the step loop.
steps.criteria: Invisible choices never appear in currentChoices even as empty text; when all visible choices are exhausted an invisible default auto-executes without requiring a chooseChoice call; visible choices always take priority over invisible defaults; no empty-text choice traps the story; all Slice 04 acceptance tests pass
steps.implementation_notes: Invisible default detection predicate: (flags & 0x10 == 0) AND (flags & 0x06 == 0) AND (flags & 0x01 == 0) — i.e. flags & 0x17 == 0. During collection if this predicate is true, store targetPath in local var pendingInvisibleDefault (keep only the first). Do not append to currentChoices. After the collection loop: if currentChoices.isEmpty, pendingInvisibleDefault is non-nil, apply the divert (update pointer/stack) and re-enter the step loop. Guard against infinite loops: pendingInvisibleDefault is consumed once and cleared. The auto-divert must also survive save/restore since the divert is applied before the state is exposed — no additional Codable changes needed.
quality_gates: story never hangs with empty currentChoices and non-nil invisible default target
no regression in Slice 01, Slice 02, Slice 03, or Tier 1 tests
deliverables: Invisible default detection predicate in collection loop
pendingInvisibleDefault local variable capturing first invisible target
Post-collection auto-divert when currentChoices is empty
